dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4888 IS: Preventing Risk and Exploitation through Validated Education and Network-based Training Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Alsobrooks (for herself and Ms. Murkowski) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions

form.official-title: To require the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to maintain and expand a program on child sexual abuse prevention.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Preventing Risk and Exploitation through Validated Education and Network-based Training Act or the PREVENT Act.

section.header: Program on child sexual abuse prevention
section.text: Title III of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 241 et seq.) is amended by inserting after section 317V of such Act (42 U.S.C. 247b–24) the following:

section.header: Program on child sexual abuse prevention

subsection.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, shall maintain and expand a program on child sexual abuse prevention.

paragraph.text: identify gaps in research and practice that are important to address in the efforts of the Federal Government to promote primary prevention of child sexual abuse; and
address such gaps and improve such research and practice, including by—

subparagraph.text: improving surveillance systems and data collection for monitoring child sexual abuse, including technology-facilitated child sexual abuse;
increasing understanding of risk and protective factors for child sexual abuse perpetration and victimization;
strengthening existing, and developing new, evidence-based policies, programs, and practices for the primary prevention of child sexual abuse;
increasing dissemination and implementation of evidence-based strategies for child sexual abuse prevention; and
increasing understanding of the rise in technology-facilitated child sexual abuse and development of evidence-based strategies to address such abuse.

subsection.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $6,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.
